title: Rial Breaches 1,420,000/USD Amid Panic Selling
detail: The rial fell to 1,420,000 per US dollar on the open market as panic selling intensified. Currency exchange shops in Tehran reported being overwhelmed with sellers. The gap between the official rate (42,000) and the open market rate widened to a factor of 34x, the largest divergence in Iran's history.

title: Social Tensions Rise Across Provincial Cities
detail: Reports emerged of localized protests in Kermanshah, Ahvaz, and Zahedan over food prices and the economic crisis. Small groups of residents gathered outside provincial governor offices demanding action. Security forces maintained a heavy presence but avoided direct confrontation.

title: IMF Downgrades Iran Economic Outlook
detail: The International Monetary Fund revised Iran's 2026 GDP growth forecast from -2.1% to -4.8%, citing the currency collapse, intensified sanctions enforcement, and declining oil revenue. The report warned of potential humanitarian consequences if food import financing collapsed alongside the rial.
